content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks that rely on single-perspective iterative self-feedback can be enhanced by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives at the core of recent LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective self-refine framework that avoids the common pitfall of locking into local optima from the LLM’s initial biased output. This hybrid framework retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of requiring no additional training or labeled data, while leveraging perspective diversity to improve output quality beyond what single-perspective iteration can achieve.
